title: Francophone Immigration: Cameroon - Canada.ca
content:
## Francophone Immigration: Cameroon

## French and English Speakers Additional information - French and English speakers
Geographical distribution of the population

## Predominant sectors of activity Additional information - Predominant sectors of activity
- Agriculture, fishing and related production
- Mining, oil and gas industry
- Manufacturing Industry
- Finance – Banking – Insurance
- Trade – Sales and services
Distribution of sectors
Definition according to the World Bank: The total labour force includes people aged 15 and older who meet the International Labour Organization’s definition of the economically active population, that is, all individuals who provide labour for the production of goods and services during a given period. This definition includes both workers and job seekers. While national practices vary in the treatment of groups such as armed forces personnel and seasonal or part-time workers, in general, the labour force includes armed forces personnel, job seekers and first-time job seekers, but excludes homemakers and other caregivers and workers in the informal sector who are not paid.
8 French-speaking regions and 2 English-speaking regions
One in 10 inhabitants lives in Douala.
Education and early childhood are also important sectors, but there is some reluctance from the State regarding recruitment in this sector.
        Health presents an interesting pool, but the country is on the WHO health workforce support and safeguards list 2023 .
Primary sector: all economic activities producing raw materials (agriculture, forestry, fisheries and, to some extent, mining)
Secondary sector comprises all economic activities corresponding to the transformation of raw materials into productive or consumer goods. (This is the industrial sector.)
Tertiary sector: sector of economic activities including trade, transport, services, etc.
The information contained in these pages is subject to change. Updates will be made as often as possible.
